behaviors.description: Error message names missing required key by exact config path when key is absent from both file and environment
behaviors.justification: The failure is purely about error message formatting and validation logic. A mock config dict missing a required key will exercise the same validation path as a real file would. The error message generation itself has no file-system dependencies. Unit tests can catch wrong key naming, missing key notation, or unclear messages by providing test inputs to the loader's validation function.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a config dict (mocked file load) missing a required key like 'database.host', with no TOOL_DATABASE_HOST environment variable. Call loader and verify error message contains 'database.host' in the same notation format.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test only exercises validation logic, not file I/O; no mock-hiding of real failures expected here.

behaviors.description: Loader resolves config file path relative to XDG_CONFIG_HOME when environment variable is set, not ~/.config
behaviors.justification: A mock of os.environ.get or os.path.expanduser could return correct values without the code actually using them. Real failure mode: code always uses ~/.config and ignores XDG_CONFIG_HOME. Only actual file I/O reveals this—if loader reads from wrong location, it fails to find the file or reads stale config. Must test with real path resolution and file operations to catch this silent failure.
behaviors.test_description: Create temp directory, set XDG_CONFIG_HOME to it, write a config file in that location with a unique sentinel value, invoke loader, verify sentinel is present. Also verify loader does NOT fall back to ~/.config when XDG_CONFIG_HOME is set.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires actual environment variable handling + file I/O; mocking either would miss the real failure.

behaviors.description: Environment variable with TOOL_ prefix overrides conflicting config file value
behaviors.justification: The failure is about merge logic: does the code check environment variables, do they override correctly, is the TOOL_ prefix mapping correct (TOOL_DATABASE_HOST → database.host). All testable in isolation with mocked file I/O. Mocking the config file read and providing test environment variables (or mocking os.environ.get) exercises the same code path as reading a real file would. Real failure would be caught: env var not checked, or checked but override doesn't apply.
behaviors.test_description: Mock file load to return config with database.host='file-value'. Set TOOL_DATABASE_HOST='env-value' in environment (or provide mock). Invoke loader and verify merged result has database.host='env-value'.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test exercises merge logic only; file I/O not relevant to this specific failure mode.

behaviors.description: YAML syntax error message includes the line number where problem was detected
behaviors.justification: Mocking the YAML parser to raise an exception could fail to catch real issues—YAML library error message formats are fragile and version-dependent. Real failure mode: code doesn't extract line number from YAML exception, or exception message format changed. Must use actual YAML parsing (with real parser) to be confident line number extraction works. Can use in-memory YAML string (StringIO) rather than file, but must parse with real library.
behaviors.test_description: Create invalid YAML with syntax error on a specific line (e.g., unmatched bracket on line 5). Attempt to load with real YAML parser. Verify error message includes the line number.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real YAML parsing library; mocking would hide fragility in error-message parsing logic.

behaviors.description: Loader creates config directory and writes defaults file on first run when directory does not exist
behaviors.justification: Mocking os.makedirs and file write would hide real failures: nested directory creation, permission issues, default file format problems, or file written to wrong location. Real failure mode: code doesn't create all parent directories, or fails on missing permissions, or defaults file is malformed. Must test with actual file system (temporary directory) to verify directory is created, defaults file exists and is loadable.
behaviors.test_description: Use tempfile to create isolated temp directory. Point loader config path to non-existent subdirectory within it. Invoke loader. Verify: (1) directory and all parent paths are created, (2) defaults file exists in that directory, (3) defaults file is valid YAML and loadable.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real file system I/O with temp directory; mocking would miss directory-creation edge cases and file-format issues.
